title: Complexity/length ladder ceiling
method: one scenario at a time in tmux 0:2.5, poll ~30s, UX analysis

ladder.product: Found EN→PT language bug; fixed with language lock
pt-BR solid; genericity requires user pushback
Best multi-turn pattern: inject natal, one transit, short memory
SOFT cost ceiling opens: year ~21k tok
Recovery+lang switch OK; ~8k tok even for tool-free turns
COST+FIDELITY ceiling: double transit windows, invented exact dates

ceilings.hard_fail: Not reached at 7 turns / 80k tokens — assertions still pass

latency_ux.signal: >30s wait before first token (chat feels broken)

fidelity.signal: model admits tool is near-term snapshot then invents seasons/exact dates
fidelity.risk: product may present approximate dates as if computed

context_bloat.signal: one-word answer costs ~12.8k tokens (almost all prompt)

reply_length.signal: 5–6k char walls; mobile Diário hard to scan

product_recommendations: Default Diário flows stay in u1–u4 envelope (daily + short follow-ups)
Year views: dedicated UI with precomputed seasons; do not raw-dump 30 aspects into chat
Transit tool: return seasonal summaries for windows >30d; never invent exact dates without ephemeris
Streaming + skeleton UI for any tool path expected >8s
History compaction after tool-heavy turns (drop raw tool JSON from later prompts)
Keep language lock; lead with one concrete chart fact before prose
Synastry empty-state (ask partner chart) already good UX — keep
sweet_spot: 2–5 turns, ≤1 transit tool, ≤35k tokens, ≤60s total — u1/u4 pattern
ceiling_call: Product ceiling is at multi-month transit + long thread (u5–u7), not hard crash. Ship for short/medium; redesign year path before marketing year-ahead chat.
